content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Diane Elan wick 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
February 10, 1988 
650 California street, Suite 2650 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Dear Ms. wick: 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-88-033 
You have requested advice under the campa~gn provisions of 
the Political Reform Act of 1974 (the "Act").1:1 
Because your 
question was presented in a hypothetical manner, we can not 
provide formal advice. 
(Regulation l8329(b) (8) (d).) 
However, 
we can provide general guidance to assist you in analyzing the 
situation.V 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
Does section 84305(b) apply to a general purpose 
recipient committee, a sponsored committee, or a committee that 
is controlled by only one candidate, and does Section 84305(a) 
apply to a committee that is controlled by more than one 
candidate? 
2. 
Is it necessary for the name of a sponsored committee to 
appear on the inserts of a mass mailing sent by the committee as 
long as the full name of the committee appears on the outside of 
the envelope? 
3. 
Do envelopes printed for a mass mailing which have the 
full name and address of the sponsored committee sending the 
mailing on the back flap and an unrelated name and address in 
the upper-left corner of the front of the envelope meet with the 
mass mailing requirements set forth in section 84305? 
!/Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
re 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California 
of Regulations section 18000, et seg. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of 
Regul 
assi 
not provide the requestor 
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Section 83114; 
ation 18329(C) (3), copy enclosed.) 
.. P.O. Box 807 .. Sacramento CA 95804~0807 .. (916)3 


wick, Diane Elan 
page Two 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. 
section 84305(a) relates to all candidates and 
committees which send a mass mailing. 
The term "single 
candidate or committee," as found in section 84305(b), relates 
to a candidate or committee which is the only "sender" of a mass 
mailing. 
2. 
As long as the sponsored committee is the only "sender" 
of a mass mailing (i.e., the only candidate or committee which 
pays for the mailing) and the full name of the sponsored 
committee and its address appear on the outside of the envelope, 
the name of the sponsored committee need not appear on any 
insert. 
3. 
As long as the full name of the sponsored committee and 
its address appear in no less than six-point type on the outside 
of the envelope, additional information on the envelope is not 
prohibited. 
FACTS 
You represent a sponsored committee which is primarily 
formed to oppose a ballot measure. 
The committee intends to 
send a mass mailing, as defined by Section 82041.5, consisting 
of a fundraising letter by President Gerald Ford on his personal 
stationery. 
The full name of the committee and its sponsor will 
be printed on the outside of the back flap of the envelope. 
Mr. 
Ford's name and address will be printed in the upper-left hand 
corner of the front of the envelope. 
ANALYSIS 
1. 
section 84305(a) outlines the requirements for a mass 
mailing sent by any candidate, combination of candidates, 
committee or combination of committees. 
The name, street 
address, and city of the candidate(s) or committee(s) must be on 
the outside of each niece of mail and on at least one insert. 
This identification must be in no less than 6-point type. 
section 84305(b) eliminates the requirement for 
identification on an insert for a mass mailing sent by a 
"single" candidate committee. 
A mailing is sent by a single 
candidai:e or committee if all the costs attributable to the 
mailing are paid by only one candidate or committee. 
(Regulation 18435.) 


Wick, Diane Elan 
Page Three 
2. 
See Analysis #1, section 84305(b). 
3. 
The Act does not state whether addresses of persons 
other than the sender can be included on the outside of the 
envelope of a mass mailing. 
Therefore, inclusion of Mr. Ford's 
name and address is not prohibited. 
In addition, the Act does not specify where on the outside 
of the envelope the sponsored committee's name and address must 
appear, only that it be in no less than 6-point type. 
The fact 
that Mr. Ford's name and address will be on the front and the 
sponsored committee's name and address will be on the back does 
not violate the mass mailing provisions of the Act. 
If you have further questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5662. 
By: 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Co 
sel 
~in S. Braaten-Moen 
~61itical Reform Consultant 


LAW OFFICES OF 
NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER 
SACRAMENTO 
1030 FI FTEENTH STREET, SUITE 250 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 
TELEPHONE (916) 446,6752 
Sacramento 
Dear 
disc 
A PARTNERSHIP INCLlJDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS 
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108 
TELEPHONE (415) 989'6800 
1 
f 
1988 
FILE NUM8ER 
to confirm 
by you on 
to Government Code section 84305 and the 
mass-mailings. 
, a sponsored 
led by

13, 1988 
please confirm that 
above advice is 
correct by s

and 
it to me 
as soon as possible. 
Very 
yours,

above 
acknowledged as correct:

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Diane Elan wick 
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson, 
Parrinello & Mueller 
January 15, 1988 
650 California street, Suite 2605 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Re: 
88-033 
Dear Ms. wick: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on January 14, 1888 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
/7 
I

iJeanne Pritchard 
Chief

II 
and Analysis 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacram,cnto CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660
